work-item-template-creation.title: Create Azure DevOps Work Item Templates

description_sections.heading: User story
description_sections.body: As a CAF delivery team member, I want reusable Azure Boards work item templates for the Agile work item types used by the project, so that new Features, User Stories, Tasks, Bugs, and Issues are created consistently with the right field guidance, naming conventions, tags, and acceptance criteria.

description_sections.heading: Context
bullets: Use 00 - Inbox\Azure DevOps Agile Work Item Types.md as the work item type guidance.
Use Microsoft Learn guidance for Azure Boards work item templates: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/devops/boards/backlogs/work-item-template?view=azure-devops&tabs=browser
Create templates in the Azure DevOps web portal under Project settings > Boards > Team configuration > Templates.

description_sections.heading: Scope
bullets: Review the Agile work item types used by this project: Feature, User Story, Task, Bug, and Issue.
Create template definitions for work item types repeatedly used by the CAF backlog.
Prepopulate common fields where appropriate, such as tags, area path, description prompts, acceptance criteria prompts, activity, priority, and risk.
Capture or create shareable template links and document where the team can find them.
Document template limitations, including that web portal templates are managed and applied through the web portal.

acceptance_criteria: Work item templates exist for the agreed common CAF work item types, or a decision is recorded for any type intentionally excluded.
Each template includes clear title guidance, description guidance, and the relevant default tags/fields.
User Story templates include acceptance criteria prompts aligned to the Agile process guidance.
Task templates include implementation guidance and, where useful, activity/remaining work prompts.
Bug and Issue templates clearly distinguish defects from blockers, risks, or impediments.
Template links are captured in a team-accessible location such as the project wiki, dashboard, or team documentation.
A test work item can be created from at least one template and an existing work item can have a template applied successfully.

feature.title: <Capability Name>

description_sections.heading: Capability
description_sections.body: Describe the capability or product outcome this feature delivers.

acceptance_criteria: The capability is clearly decomposed into child user stories.
Completion can be demonstrated or evidenced.

user-story.title: <Deliver user or stakeholder outcome>

description_sections.heading: User story
description_sections.body: As a <user/stakeholder>, I want <capability/change>, so that <business or operational value>.

description_sections.heading: Context
description_sections.body: Add enough background for the team to estimate, plan, and validate the work.

acceptance_criteria: Acceptance criteria are specific, testable, and agreed by the product owner.
Relevant validation, pipeline, or manual test evidence is captured.

description_sections.heading: Task
description_sections.body: Describe the concrete implementation, documentation, investigation, or testing activity.

description_sections.heading: Done when
bullets: Relevant evidence is linked to the parent work item.
Remaining work is updated before closure.

bug.title: Fix <defective behaviour>

description_sections.heading: Problem
description_sections.body: Describe the observed defective product behaviour.

acceptance_criteria: The defect is reproducible or enough evidence exists to diagnose it.
The fix is implemented and validated.
Regression risk is considered and covered where appropriate.

description_sections.heading: Issue
description_sections.body: Describe the blocker, risk, dependency, or non-code impediment affecting delivery.

description_sections.heading: Impact
description_sections.body: Explain what work is blocked or at risk.
